item.title: The death of Bay Area public transportation
item.description: Massive service cuts loom despite a surge in local government spending.

item.title: Religious education hypocrisy in Texas
item.description: Punishing Muslim schools will inevitably backfire.

item.title: Gavin Newsom’s taxpayer-funded PR blitz
item.description: The California governor should know that throwing money at a problem doesn’t mean better outcomes.

item.title: South Dakota joins the lab-grown meat panic
item.description: People can decide for themselves what they’d like to eat.

item.title: Dressing for unpredictable spring weather
item.description: Spin the outfit generator!

item.title: Guess who’s asking for DEI now
item.description: Evangelicals, Easter, Ramadan and humanism, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: The ‘nonprofit hospital’ charade
item.description: Charitable tax deductions, autism and Adam Smith, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: Cartoon by Clay Bennett
item.description: Clay Bennett cartoon on gas prices and the war in Iran.

item.title: Iran is an imperial trap. America walked right in.
item.description: Trump’s decision to return to the Middle East echoes the strategic folly that undid Britain.

item.title: James Talarico represents Christianity’s past, not its future
item.description: The Texas Democrat’s support for transgender rights helps explain why liberal Christianity is dying.

item.title: America needs immigrants as much as they need liberty’s blessings
item.description: When a waiter of 20 years is deported, suddenly immigration has a face and gains complexity.

item.title: The GOP is having an epiphany: This is a bad look
item.description: MAGA plans a makeover — just in time for the midterms.

item.title: The game is up in Venezuela
item.description: Ten weeks after Nicolás Maduro’s capture, “interim authorities” need to move toward democracy.

item.title: What is America’s role in international organizations? Take our quiz.
item.description: A quiz to test your knowledge of global alliances, peacekeeping and the U.S. role in it all.

item.title: Gavin Newsom missed the memo: Relatability is out
item.description: California’s governor has written a “man of the people” memoir. Read the room.

item.title: Three cheers for scuttling the Jones Act
item.description: The 100-year-old protectionist law deserves more than a temporary waiver.

item.title: Who was behind Iranian girls' school bombing?
item.description: An upcoming Washington Post Opinions show. Stay tuned.

item.title: Victory?
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on commander in chief Trump, napalm and oil tankers.

item.title: A euphemism for the ages in Virginia
item.description: The bipartisan redistricting rush of 2026 is ennobling neither party.

item.title: Washington state’s tax folly
item.description: Democrats have voted to bring in a state income levy for residents earning over $1 million a year.

item.title: Universities should let students speak
item.description: Plus, readers on investing in infrastructure and war-proofing your budget.

item.title: Why are ultra-processed foods so bad? Answering your questions.
item.description: A dietitian explains why it’s best to limit ultra-processed food.
